tonkori.subtitle: Would you rather save the world... or save yourself?
tonkori.description: Tonkori is Rhon's flagship title, a 3D narrative RPG we've been developing since 2024. You are Scrap, a nobody from Uranus, the poorest and most looked-down-upon settlement in Khal'Loth, dragged by accident into an expedition meant to end in failure, one that instead becomes the first crack in a conspiracy reaching all the way to the highest seat of human civilization.
tonkori.description2: Your choices shape the story, your relationships with companions, and the way the world reacts to you. No story is the same, only the one you choose.
tonkori.pitch: Your decisions change the world and your relationships with your companions.

mechanics.title: Choices with real weight
mechanics.description: Every choice, who you are or how you behave, redefines the story, the relationships, and the world of Arceon around you.

mechanics.title: Companions who remember
mechanics.description: Companions remember everything. They can become allies, lovers, or enemies depending on how you treat them.

mechanics.title: Origin system
mechanics.description: Five distinct origins (Khalnoith, Vaelen, Grunthor, Ashford, Enkurok) are different lenses on the same events, not difficulty settings. None of them is the 'correct' path.

market.title: Competitive Edge
market.description: Tonkori doesn't compete with fast-paced action. It targets players looking to immerse themselves in a warm, handcrafted three-dimensional world, with a deep story and companions who genuinely respond to their decisions. The combination of a narrative system with real emotional weight and a carefully crafted setting sets it apart from generic RPGs in the indie market.

market.title: Tags
market.description: 3D narrative RPG · Choices with consequences · No predetermined endings · Dynamic companions · Developed in Spanish · Passionate indie studio

technic.title: Engine
technic.description: To be determined - Evaluating different 3D engines to achieve the desired experience

technic.title: Languages
technic.description: Spanish (native language) + English confirmed. Voice acting planned in Spanish as the main language

technic.title: Monetization
technic.description: One-time purchase (premium) with/or without Early Access + full release

development.current_phase: On hold - Consolidating the Narrative Bible before resuming production (-8% complete)

tonkori.vision: A 3D RPG where every decision matters, companions remember, and the story you live is uniquely yours. There are no right paths, only consequences.

items: Act structure and key plot turns
Playable demo of the first act showcasing the full experience: narrative, companions, combat, and decisions
Evaluation of launching in early access to gather community feedback before the full release.
Official, final launch on PC (Steam) and console platforms.

investment.monetization: One-time purchase (premium) or Early Access followed by full release. Final decision to be made after evaluating community feedback once the prototype is live.
investment.roi: Narrative RPG market growing steadily at 12% annually. Appetite for titles with deep companion systems and strong artistic identity keeps rising within the indie segment.
keys: Solid concept with clear differentiation in the indie narrative 3D RPG market.
Small, committed team with complementary skill sets (narrative + programming/art)
Studio's first major project: greater agility and no accumulated technical debt.
Active target audience willing to pay for quality narrative experiences.
Developed in Spanish, the native language of a massive and underrepresented gaming market.

afterlight.subtitle: A Wordless Story Between Light and Darkness
afterlight.description: AfterLight tells the story of Lumen, a being of light who rescues Umbra, a being of darkness trapped and weakened in the middle region of the world. Lumen decides to accompany Umbra back to his homeland, even knowing the journey will weaken him in return.
afterlight.description2: A platforming and exploration game that conveys calmness and melancholy without dialogue, allowing music and visual changes to tell the story of two opposing beings learning to help one another throughout their journey.
afterlight.pitch: Light and shadow cross the world together: the stronger one becomes, the weaker the other grows.

mechanics.title: Fluid Movement and Combat
mechanics.description: Lumen can jump, perform directional dashes, and use a contextual attack/interaction, allowing players to combine abilities to dodge and strike the Forsaken and hostile wildlife with precision.

mechanics.title: Light and Darkness Puzzles
mechanics.description: The environment features lanterns, portable flames, and breakable branches that players must activate, extinguish, or carry to reveal hidden paths and progress through each area.

mechanics.title: Narrative Inversion Across Zones
mechanics.description: Throughout the game's five regions, Lumen gradually loses agility and strength while Umbra regains them, translating their relationship of mutual support directly into gameplay.

market.title: Target Audience
market.description: Players aged 14–35 interested in slow-paced narrative experiences without dialogue or explicit violence.

market.title: Market Size
market.description: Narrative platformer and metroidvania niche on PC (Steam), a segment with a proven loyal audience.

market.title: Competitive Advantage
market.description: A strength-transfer mechanic directly tied to the narrative, combined with a hand-painted visual identity inspired by MIO: Memories in Orbit.

development.current_phase: Game Design Documentation (GDD) and Concept Art

afterlight.vision: AfterLight aims to tell an intimate story about sacrifice and friendship between two opposing beings without using words. Through music, hand-painted art, and the gradual physical transformation of the characters, players experience firsthand how the balance between strength and weakness shifts throughout the journey.

items: Definition of core mechanics and narrative arc
Concept art for characters and enemies
Enemy AI and Umbra AI implementation
Puzzle systems based on light and darkness
Creation of the five narrative regions (White to Black)

investment.monetization: Premium one-time purchase on PC via Steam
investment.roi: Narrative indie niche with potential comparable to successes such as Hollow Knight and Neva
keys: Distinctive hand-painted visual identity inspired by MIO: Memories in Orbit
Dialogue-free storytelling conveyed through mechanics, music, and art

theobserver.subtitle: Otome Mystery & Suspense Visual Novel
theobserver.description: The Observer is an otome visual novel where you choose one of five characters to accompany, and your story always saves at the moment they die. Once tragedy strikes, you travel back several days to prevent it.
theobserver.description2: Discover who is killing them, keep their trust without looking insane, and decide the killer's fate. Every death you prevent grants experience to improve your Charisma, Manipulation, and more. Will you earn the ending you deserve... or doom everyone to relive the same story?
theobserver.pitch: The save point is always their death. You decide whether that changes.

mechanics.title: Death Loop & Time Rewind
mechanics.description: The game automatically saves the moment your love interest dies. From there, you can rewind several days and act using the knowledge you've already gained to change the outcome.

mechanics.title: Undercover Investigation
mechanics.description: Uncover the killer's identity without revealing you know too much. Telling the truth to your companion may cause them to ignore you—or something even worse. Explore, talk to people, and gradually unlock clues.

mechanics.title: Influence Stats
mechanics.description: Every death you prevent grants experience to improve Charisma (to make others listen) or Manipulation (to control situations). Your love interest may reject you depending on how you've used these abilities.

market.title: Target Audience
market.description: Players aged 16 and up who enjoy otome visual novels and narrative mystery games such as Raging Loop and Paranormasight.

market.title: Market Size
market.description: The otome and PC visual novel genre has a loyal and active audience on Steam, with indie titles often reaching tens of thousands of sales despite modest budgets.

market.title: Competitive Advantage
market.description: Its combination of time-rewind mechanics, multiple endings influenced by player stats, and a fragile trust system sets it apart from traditional otome games.

theobserver.vision: An otome where romance coexists with the horror of knowing the person you love is destined to die—and that you're the only one who can stop it, over and over again, without anyone believing you.

items: Core premise and gameplay loop defined
Romanceable cast outlined (Julian, Kieran, Ivy, Luna + 1)
Automatic save system at the moment of death
All 5 complete routes with unique killers

investment.monetization: Premium one-time purchase on Steam for PC. Early Access may be considered to gather community feedback before full release.
investment.roi: The otome genre on Steam has an active fanbase willing to pay for high-quality experiences. Indie titles like Cinderella Phenomenon demonstrate that original concepts can reach large audiences with small teams.
keys: Unique time-rewind mechanic within the otome genre
Stat system that rewards or penalizes player choices
5 routes with different killers provide high replayability
Dedicated target audience on digital platforms
Committed team with a strong narrative and visual identity

tinycare.subtitle: A Virtual Pet with Real Consequences
tinycare.description: Tiny Care is a mobile virtual pet game where every decision truly matters. Your pet isn't just a cute sprite—it eats, sleeps, gets dirty, becomes bored, develops its own personality, and can even die if you fail to care for it properly.
tinycare.description2: Choose from 14 species with different rarity levels, from cats and dogs to axolotls and owls, and care for them throughout every stage of their lives. Neglect their mental health and you'll notice signs of sadness; ignore their nutrition and they'll suffer the consequences. Are you responsible enough to keep them alive?
tinycare.pitch: It's more than a pet game. It's a reminder of what it means to care for someone.

mechanics.title: Interconnected Needs System
mechanics.description: Hunger, sleep, hygiene, and happiness influence one another in real time. Playing too much tires your pet, while neglecting it lowers your bond. Every action has consequences.

mechanics.title: Dynamic Personality
mechanics.description: Every pet is born with fixed personality traits (Materialistic, Playful, Silly) and can develop new ones depending on how you raise it. Neglect its emotional well-being and it may become Aggressive, Fearful, or Clingy.

mechanics.title: 14 Species with Rarity & Life Cycle
mechanics.description: From common pets (cats, dogs, rabbits) to SSR creatures (hedgehogs, owls, axolotls), every species has its own lifespan—from two weeks to a month and a half—and will eventually die of old age if properly cared for.

market.title: Target Audience
market.description: Players aged 16 and up who enjoy virtual pet games such as Tamagotchi, Tsuki Odyssey, or Can Your Pet? and are looking for greater depth and meaningful consequences.

market.title: Market Size
market.description: The casual mobile life-simulation market generates billions in annual revenue. Tiny Care differentiates itself by adding emotional depth beyond traditional virtual pet games.

market.title: Competitive Advantage
market.description: Unlike conventional pet games, Tiny Care features real consequences for neglect, including depression-like behavior, health problems, and death, creating a genuine emotional bond between player and pet.

technic.title: Genre
technic.description: Life Simulation · Virtual Pet · Collection · Raising Sim

technic.title: Planned Tools
technic.description: TBD - Designed for native mobile development

tinycare.vision: A virtual pet game that makes you feel what it truly means to care for a living being: the joy of watching it grow, the guilt of neglecting it, and the weight of every decision you make on its behalf.

items: Concept art for all 14 species and life stages
Mobile UI design (main screen and status indicators)
Playable pet with core gameplay loop (hunger, sleep, happiness)
All 14 animated species with full life stages
Complete personality, health, and mood systems
Shop, outfits, skins, and gift system
Original soundtrack and pet sound effects
Testing on Android and iOS devices
Google Play and App Store release

investment.monetization: TBD. Options under evaluation include free-to-play with cosmetic purchases or a hybrid model. Clothing, skins, and gifts provide natural, non-intrusive monetization opportunities.
investment.roi: The virtual pet market has proven demand (Tamagotchi Uni, Pou, Tsuki Odyssey). Tiny Care stands out through its emotional depth, real consequences, mental health mechanics, and permanent loss, appealing to players looking for a more meaningful casual experience.
keys: Real consequences create a genuine emotional bond
14 species with varying rarities encourage collection and replayability
Dynamic personalities make every pet unique
Large and active mobile simulation audience
Distinctive visual style inspired by high-quality artistic references
